questions.prompt: The graph of a piecewise-linear function \(f\), for \(-1\le x\le4\), is shown. What is \(\displaystyle\int_{-1}^{4}f(x)\,dx\)?

questions.prompt: If \(f\) is continuous on \([a,b]\) and differentiable on \((a,b)\), which statement could be false?
questions.explanation: The Mean Value Theorem guarantees choice A, and continuity on a closed interval guarantees maxima, minima, and integrability. A horizontal tangent is not guaranteed unless additional conditions such as \(f(a)=f(b)\) hold. A nonconstant linear function is a counterexample.

questions.prompt: Let \(f\) and \(g\) be differentiable, with \(g(x)>0\) for all \(x\) and \(f(0)=1\). If \(h(x)=f(x)g(x)\) and \(h\prime(x)=f(x)g\prime(x)\), then \(f(x)=\)
questions.explanation: The product rule gives \(h\prime=f\prime g+fg\prime\). Comparing with the given equation yields \(f\prime(x)g(x)=0\). Since \(g(x)>0\), \(f\prime(x)=0\), so \(f\) is constant. Because \(f(0)=1\), \(f(x)=1\).
questions.workedSolution: The product rule gives \(h\prime=f\prime g+fg\prime\). Comparing with the given equation yields \(f\prime(x)g(x)=0\). Since \(g(x)>0\), \(f\prime(x)=0\), so \(f\) is constant. Because \(f(0)=1\), \(f(x)=1\).

questions.prompt: The velocity is \(v(t)=t^3-3t^2+12t+4\). What is the maximum acceleration on \(0\le t\le3\)?
questions.explanation: Acceleration is \(a(t)=v\prime(t)=3t^2-6t+12\). This upward-opening quadratic has its minimum at \(t=1\), so the maximum on the closed interval is at an endpoint. \(a(0)=12\) and \(a(3)=27-18+12=21\).

questions.prompt: What is the average value of \(y=x^2\sqrt{x^3+1}\) on \([0,2]\)?
questions.explanation: The average value is \(\frac12\int_0^2x^2\sqrt{x^3+1}\,dx\). Let \(u=x^3+1\), so \(du=3x^2dx\). Then the value is \(\frac16\int_1^9u^{1/2}du=\frac19[u^{3/2}]_1^9=\frac19(27-1)=\frac{26}{9}\).

questions.prompt: Let \(f(x)=3e^{2x}\) and \(g(x)=6x^3\). At what value of \(x\) do their graphs have parallel tangent lines?

questions.prompt: If \(f\prime(x)=\dfrac{\cos^2x}{x}-\dfrac15\), how many critical values does \(f\) have on \((0,10)\)?

questions.prompt: If \(g(x)<0\) for all real \(x\) and \(f\prime(x)=(x^2-4)g(x)\), which statement is true?

choices.label: A

choices.label: B
choices.text: f has a relative minimum at -2 and a relative maximum at 2.

choices.label: C
